artPrompt: A gaunt fire elemental flickers into being half-formed, its flame-orange body riddled with three gaping voids that drink the surrounding light, shoulders slumped in cheerful denial as embers leak from the holes, foreshortened looming over a feral town square. Overturned ballot boxes and a bonfire glow behind in night-black smoke, sparks and dice scattering, comic existential decay. Rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: The Minus-Three Elemental
text:
The Minus-Three Elemental enters the battlefield with -3/-3, which is fine, this is fine.
Whenever The Minus-Three Elemental attacks, it deals negative damage to the defending player, so they gain 3 life and feel bad about it.
Gain -1 life whenever this dies. (Yes, that means you gain a life. No, don't think about it.)
flavorText: It owes the battlefield three creatures and is not paying them back.

extras.impossibleMath: negative power/toughness, negative damage, gain -1 life
